mnemonics: FURPS Model: Functionality, Usability, Reliability, Performance, Supportability
keyPoints: Quality is conformance to requirements, not just the absence of defects.
Quality assurance is a proactive process, while quality control is reactive (testing).
A formal SQA (Software Quality Assurance) plan is essential for managing quality activities throughout the project lifecycle.
commonMistakes: Focusing solely on functional testing while neglecting non-functional requirements (performance, security, usability).
Treating quality as a separate phase at the end of the project instead of integrating it throughout the entire SDLC.
